content.problemStatement:
Given an integer array numsnumsnums where **every number appears exactly twice except for two distinct numbers**, your task is to **identify the two numbers that occur only once**.
All other numbers appear in pairs.
Return the two unique numbers **in ascending order**.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindSingleNumbersfindSingleNumbersfindSingleNumbers â€“ This function determines the two integers that appear exactly once in an array where all other integers appear twice.

Parameters
numsnumsnums: A list of integers representing the array elements.
Return Value
Returns a list of **two integers** that appear only once, sorted in **ascending order**.
`The input and output formats below are only if you want to test using custom inputs.`

Constraints
2â‰¤nums.lengthâ‰¤1052 \leq nums.length \leq 10^52â‰¤nums.lengthâ‰¤105
âˆ’3Ã—105â‰¤nums[i]â‰¤3Ã—105-3 \times 10^5 \leq nums[i] \leq 3 \times 10^5âˆ’3Ã—105â‰¤nums[i]â‰¤3Ã—105
Exactly two integers appear once; all others appear twice.

Input Format


The first line contains a single integer TTT â€” the number of test cases.




Each test case consists of:



A single line containing comma-separated integers representing the array numsnumsnums.




Output Format

For each test case, print the two unique integers in ascending order inside brackets.
Example:
[a,b][a, b][a,b]

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
For first test case: Every element appears twice except 9 and 11, which appear only once.
For second test case: -5 appears twice, while -2 and 13 appear exactly once.
